texto: How can I get up everyday knowing you had to kill yourself to make it stop hurting and I was here all the time and I never even saw it. And then you gave me this chance to make it better, convince you to stay alive and I couldn't do it. How can I live with myself after this, Jessie?

Ao questionar “How can I get up everyday knowing you had to kill yourself to make it stop hurting and I was here…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, play, everyday, com foco em reflexao.

texto: Mama, I know you used to ride the bus. Riding the bus and it’s hot and bumpy and crowded and too noisy and more than anything in the world you want to get off and the only reason in the world you don’t get off is it’s still fifty blocks from where you’re going? Well, I can get off right now if I want to, because even if I ride fifty more years and get off then, it’s the same place when I step down to it. Whenever I feel like it, I can get off. As soon as I’ve had enough, it’s my stop. I’ve had enough.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marsha Norman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Mama, I know you used to ride the bus. Riding the bus and it’s hot and bumpy and crowded and too …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, boredom, bus pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bus e bus-stop.

texto: No. You can't. And I can't do anything either, about my life, to change it, make it better, make me feel better about it. Like it better, make it work. But I can stop it. Shut it down, turn it off like the radio when there's nothing on I want to listen to. It's all I really have that belongs to me and I'm going to say what happens to it. And it's going to stop. And I'm going to stop it. So. Let's just have a good time.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marsha Norman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “No. You can't. And I can't do anything either, about my life, to change it, make it better, make …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, boredom, ennui pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e trabalho.

texto: I see it on his face. I hear it when he talks. We look out at the world and we see the same thing: Not Fair. And the only difference between us is Ricky's out there trying to get even. And he knows not trust anybody and he got it straight from me. And he knows not to try and get work, and guess where he got that. He walks around like there's loose boards in the floor, and you know who laid that floor, I did.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marsha Norman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a vida. O trecho “I see it on his face. I hear it when he talks. We look out at the world and we see the same thing…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, doubt, trust pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e vida.

And he knows not to try and get work, and guess where he got that. He walks around like there's loose boards in the floor, and you know who laid that floor, I did.
